tldr: Apphud manages in-app subscriptions and paywalls, while Fyber (Digital Turbine) is an ad monetization and mediation platform. Migrating means swapping a subscription revenue model for an ad-based one, which is a strategic pivot rather than a like-for-like SDK replacement.
why_switch: Pivot from subscription to ad-based monetization
Add rewarded video and offerwall for free-tier users
Tap FairBid mediation across multiple ad networks
Reduce reliance on a single subscription billing SDK
what_to_export: List of paywall placements and their app contexts
Active subscription products and price tiers
A/B test variants and their results
App Store Connect / Play Console product references
Any custom paywall designs or assets
export_method: Apphud exposes subscription, user, and event data through its dashboard and API. Use the dashboard to export subscriber lists and revenue reports as CSV, and pull historical events via the documented API where available. Capture screenshots or notes of A/B test configurations, paywall variants, and segmentation rules — these are usually not exportable as a single archive. Save webhook URLs and any server-side integration code for reference. Because the migration to Fyber is a model change rather than a data port, treat this export as documentation for your future ad-driven product spec rather than an import package. Keep the Apphud account active during the transition so historical subscriber data stays accessible for refunds, support, and accounting.
import_method: Fyber onboarding is sales-led: contact Digital Turbine to set up a publisher account, then integrate the Fyber SDK in your iOS or Android app following the official integration docs. Configure ad placements (rewarded video, interstitial, banner, offerwall) in the Fyber dashboard and map them to the screens that previously hosted Apphud paywalls. If you keep subscriptions alongside ads, leave the Apphud SDK in place and add Fyber as a parallel monetization layer. Use Fyber's mediation tools to add other demand sources, set floor prices, and run waterfall or in-app bidding. Validate fill rate and eCPM in a staging build before rolling out to production. Coordinate with your finance team on revenue reporting changes from MRR to ad revenue.

concept_mapping.note: Conceptual swap — UX and monetization model differ

gotchas: This is a business-model change, not a tool swap — validate ad ARPU vs. subscription ARPU before committing
Existing paying subscribers must be honored; do not remove the subscription path until contractually safe
App Store and Play Store policies treat ads and subscriptions differently — review compliance
Ad SDKs add binary size and may require ATT prompts and consent flows
Fyber pricing is custom; revshare details only emerge during sales conversations
Post-acquisition product roadmap under Digital Turbine may shift features

email.subject: Switching monetization from Apphud to Fyber over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are changing our monetization approach from subscriptions powered by Apphud to ad-based revenue using Fyber (Digital Turbine). Over the next 14 days we will integrate the Fyber SDK alongside our existing app, configure rewarded video, interstitial, and offerwall placements, and validate fill rate and eCPM in staging.

Existing Apphud subscribers will continue to be served on their current terms. We are not migrating subscription data into Fyber — the two platforms solve different problems. Instead, we are layering ad monetization on top, with the option to phase out paywalls later based on performance data.

Please let me know if you have questions about reporting, support, or contractual implications during the transition.

Thanks,
Your customer
